tldr: Void is an open-source AI-enabled VS Code fork used in the IDE, while CodeRabbit is an automated AI pull request reviewer that runs on your Git host. Switching means changing where AI assistance happens — from the editor to the PR — rather than transferring data.
why_switch: Your team's biggest gap is consistent code review, not in-IDE help
You want an AI tool that runs automatically on every pull request
You prefer a managed SaaS over a local editor build
You want review summaries and walkthroughs across the org
Your developers can keep using their existing IDE separately
what_to_export: Local Void editor settings and extensions list
LLM provider keys configured in Void (kept with the provider, not Void)
List of repositories or workspaces opened in Void
Any custom prompts, commands, or AI configurations
Notes or AI conversations worth preserving
export_method: Because Void is a local VS Code fork, the relevant export is your user configuration rather than a server-side dataset. Save your settings JSON, keybindings, and installed extension list. Record the LLM providers and API keys you used inside Void; these stay with the provider account when you move on. There is no centralized account to close on Void's side beyond uninstalling and revoking provider tokens. If you maintained a list of repos or workspaces, capture that list now so you can connect the same repos to CodeRabbit. Manually copy any AI conversation outputs you want to preserve into your own notes or wiki.
import_method: Install CodeRabbit as a GitHub or GitLab app on your organization or selected repositories. During onboarding, choose a plan and add the seats you need for active reviewers. For each repository, optionally commit a YAML configuration file to control review behavior, path filters, and language settings. Invite developers via your Git host's organization, then verify they appear in CodeRabbit. Open a small test pull request and confirm that automated review comments and summaries appear before rolling out broadly. Developers can continue using any IDE — Void, VS Code, or another — alongside CodeRabbit's PR-level reviews.

concept_mapping.note: Surface changes from editor to PR

concept_mapping.source: BYO LLM provider keys in Void
concept_mapping.note: CodeRabbit manages its own model access

concept_mapping.target: Repos connected to the CodeRabbit app

gotchas: CodeRabbit does not provide in-IDE code completion; developers may still want a separate IDE assistant
Self-hosted or unusual Git providers may not be supported by CodeRabbit's app
Per-repo YAML drift can lead to inconsistent reviews; centralize defaults early
Initial reviews on legacy code can be noisy until path filters and severity are tuned
Privacy expectations differ — CodeRabbit reads PR diffs in its cloud, unlike a fully local Void setup
Seat counting model may differ from your previous BYO-LLM costs; benchmark for at least one billing cycle

email.subject: Stepping away from Void in 14 days
email.body:
Hi team,

We are moving our AI-assisted code workflow toward CodeRabbit for pull request review. Void is open source so there is typically no subscription to cancel, but please treat this email as 14 days notice that we will stop using Void and revoke any related access on our side. Let me know if anything else is needed from us.

Thanks,
Your customer
